meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve no version control (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome no version control. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kits to Solve Version Control
content.seo_description: Stop losing code. Learn how to implement a SaaS starter kit to overcome version control issues using Antigravity and Claude Code in this expert case study.
content.answer_block: The root cause of 'no version control' is manual file management. By integrating a standardized SaaS starter kit, you enforce repository-first workflows. Using Antigravity to scaffold production-ready architecture combined with Claude Code to automate commit hygiene ensures that every feature you ship is tracked, versioned, and instantly deployable for clients.
content.intro:
The midnight sweat hits when you realize your client's production environment is a 'final_v2_real.zip' file hidden in a labyrinth of desktop folders. You’ve been building fast, but you’ve been building on sand. The lack of robust version control isn't just a technical oversight; it’s a ticking time bomb for your reputation and your revenue.

But the pivot from chaotic file-swapping to a professional, automated workflow is easier than you think. By adopting a hardened SaaS starter kit, you move from being a 'coder' to a 'systems architect.' This guide shows you how to leverage AI-driven tooling to bake version control directly into your development lifecycle, ensuring you never lose a line of production code again.

steps.title: Initialize Repository Foundation
steps.description: Standardize your project structure immediately to prevent drift. We use Antigravity to enforce a clean, git-ready directory structure from commit zero.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a production-grade SaaS boilerplate structure that includes a .gitignore, README.md, and a structured /src directory ready for Git initialization.

steps.title: Configure Automated Commit Hooks
steps.description: Prevent 'no version control' by automating the commit process. We set up pre-commit hooks to ensure no code hits the repo without a linting check.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a husky configuration that runs lint-staged and prettier checks before any commit is allowed.

steps.title: Implement Semantic Versioning Logic
steps.description: Stop guessing which version is live. Use automation to tag releases automatically based on your commit history.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a semantic-release configuration file that automates version tagging based on conventional commits.

steps.title: Establish Branching Strategy
steps.description: Define a clear flow for feature development vs. hotfixes to ensure your client work remains isolated and stable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a .github/workflows/main.yml file that enforces a protected main branch and requires pull request reviews for all feature branches.

steps.title: Deploy Continuous Integration
steps.description: Close the loop by connecting your repo to a CI provider so that every push triggers a build, proving your code works.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a CI/CD pipeline script using GitHub Actions that builds and tests the application on every push.

pro_tips: Always use Conventional Commits (feat:, fix:, docs:) to allow your AI tools to parse your history effectively.
Never manually edit production code on the server; if you can't push it, it doesn't exist.
Use a 'development' branch for client previews to keep the 'main' branch pristine for production releases.
Automate your changelog generation; clients love seeing a list of 'shipped' features delivered automatically.
Set up 'branch protection rules' in GitHub immediately to prevent yourself from accidentally pushing to main during late-night coding sessions.
resources: Git Best Practices for Freelancers: https://git-scm.com/doc
content.next_steps: After completing this setup, practice deploying a 'Hello World' feature using the full branch-and-merge flow to verify your automation triggers correctly.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start shipping with confidence. Our SaaS starter kit framework is designed to turn your chaotic workflow into a high-velocity machine. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
